title: How to read a game card
description: A guide to understanding all the data shown on WagerProof game prediction cards.

content.text: Each game on WagerProof is displayed as a card that packs a lot of useful information into a compact view. Here's how to read it.
The top of the card shows the two teams with their logos and records. The visiting team is listed first (left/top) and the home team second (right/bottom).
Below the matchup, you'll see rows for Spread, Moneyline, and Totals. Each row shows:

items: The current Vegas line (what sportsbooks are offering).
The model's predicted number or probability.
A value indicator showing the gap between model and market.

content.text: When the model sees a significant difference between its prediction and the Vegas line, a value tag appears. This means the model thinks the market may be mispricing that particular line. Green indicators suggest potential value bets.
Tap or click a game card to open the full game details. Here you'll find deeper analysis, betting trends, public money percentages, AI-generated insights, and more.
Pay attention to games where the model and Vegas differ significantly -- these are where the most potential value lies.
